highlights.name: Vatican City
highlights.text: Vatican City is a key city in Vatican City, combining landmarks, local culture, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: The Constantinian basilica was built in 326 over what was believed to be the tomb of Saint Peter, buried in that cemetery.

history.label: 5th century
history.text: A palace was constructed nearby as early as the 5th century during the pontificate of Pope Symmachus (reigned 498–514).
They moved to the Quirinal Palace in 1583, after work on it was completed under Pope Paul V (1605–1621).
In 1586, the obelisk was moved to its current position by Pope Sixtus V, using a method devised by the Italian architect Domenico Fontana.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: They ruled the Papal States, which covered a large portion of the Italian peninsula, for more than a thousand years until the mid-19th century, when all the territory belonging to the papacy was seized.
Their prior residence at the Quirinal Palace was taken over by the King of Italy. Under Italian rule (1871–1929) In 1870 the pope's holdings were left in an uncertain situation when Rome was annexed.
In 1871 the Quirinal Palace was confiscated by the king of Italy and became the royal palace.

The treaty, which was ratified and took effect on 7 June 1929, established the independent state of Vatican City and reaffirmed the special status of Catholic Christianity in Italy. World War II The Holy.
In connection with the Allied invasion of Sicily, 500 United States Army Air Forces aircraft bombed Rome on 19 July 1943, targeting Rome's railway hub in particular.
Pius XII created 32 cardinals in early 1946, having announced his intention to do so in his preceding Christmas message.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It was later completed by the emperor Nero and named the Circus Gaii et Neronis, usually called, simply, the Circus of Nero.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: From then on, the land mass became more populated in connection with activity at the basilica.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Lateran Palace, on the opposite side of Rome, was their habitual residence for about a thousand years.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: By the end of World War II, there were several prominent vacancies: Cardinal Secretary of State, Camerlengo, Chancellor, and Prefect for the Congregation for the Religious among them.
